pr.title: Auto-regenerate the wiki Translation Units page in CI
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Added a GitHub Actions `update-wiki` job to `.github/workflows/build.yml` that regenerates the wiki `Translation-Units.md` page from the existing `GALE01_report` artifact after successful default-branch pushes to `doldecomp/melee`. The job checks out only needed paths, installs `tools/wiki_tu.py` dependencies from `reqs/misc.txt`, clones the repository wiki using the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write`, preserves current-page data via the generator, commits only when the generated page changes, and uses a dedicated concurrency group to avoid racing wiki pushes.

key_files.reason: Added the `update-wiki` GitHub Actions job that downloads `GALE01_report`, runs `tools/wiki_tu.py`, and pushes changes to the repository wiki.

what_changed: Added an `update-wiki` job that depends on `build-ninja`, so the wiki page is regenerated only after a successful build artifact is available.
Restricted the job to pushes on the repository default branch and to `github.repository == 'doldecomp/melee'`, skipping PRs and forks.
Granted job-level permissions `contents: write` and `actions: read`, allowing the built-in GitHub token to read artifacts and push to the repo's own wiki.
Used `actions/checkout@v4` with sparse checkout for `tools` and `reqs`, minimizing checkout scope while including the generator and dependency file.
Installed generator dependencies with `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt` after review feedback replaced hardcoded package pins.
Downloaded the existing `GALE01_report` artifact into `report` instead of rebuilding or recomputing report data.
Cloned `${{ github.repository }}.wiki.git` with `https://x-access-token:${GH_TOKEN}@github.com/${repo}.wiki.git` and regenerated `Translation-Units.md` from `report/report.json`.
Wrote generated output to a temporary `.new` file, moved it into place, and used `git diff --quiet` to skip commits when the page is already current.
Configured commits as `github-actions[bot]` and pushed the wiki update to `master` with a commit message containing the source `${{ github.sha }}`.
Added a separate `concurrency` group named `update-wiki` with `cancel-in-progress: false` to prevent overlapping wiki updates from racing.
smart_moves: Reused the already-produced `GALE01_report` artifact rather than adding another build or report-generation path.
Limited execution to default-branch pushes in the canonical repository, which avoids untrusted PR/fork write attempts and unnecessary wiki churn.
Used the built-in `${{ github.token }}` plus `contents: write` instead of introducing a PAT, machine user, or GitHub App secret.
Committed only when `git diff` detected a change, keeping wiki history clean and avoiding no-op commits.
Preserved assignees by generating from the current wiki page first, according to the PR body, reducing the risk of automation overwriting human-maintained metadata.
Separated the wiki update into its own concurrency group so multiple master pushes cannot simultaneously push conflicting wiki commits.
Responded to dependency review by using the repository-maintained `reqs/misc.txt` file rather than duplicating package names in the workflow.
Verified the token and wiki-push behavior end-to-end on a fork before merge, addressing reviewer uncertainty about authentication.
decomp_lessons: For decomp progress/status pages that derive from build artifacts, wire generation into CI so wiki or documentation state does not drift from the current matching report.
Prefer consuming existing artifacts like `GALE01_report` for downstream documentation automation; it avoids redundant work and keeps the generated page tied to the validated build output.
When automating wiki writes, guard the job by event, branch, and canonical repository checks to avoid unsafe writes from PRs or forks.
Use repository dependency manifests such as `reqs/misc.txt` in CI instead of hardcoding Python package lists in workflow YAML, unless deliberately splitting dependencies into a smaller manifest.
For generated docs with human-editable fields, design generators to read the current page and preserve manual data before overwriting generated sections.
Generated documentation jobs should be idempotent: generate, diff, and skip commits if output is unchanged.
GitHub's built-in token can be sufficient for pushing to the same repository's wiki when the job has `contents: write`; extra long-lived secrets should be avoided when unnecessary.
naming_conventions: `update-wiki` was used as the workflow job id.
`Update wiki TU list` was used as the human-readable job name.
`GALE01_report` is the artifact name consumed by the wiki update job.
`Translation-Units.md` is the wiki page filename generated by `tools/wiki_tu.py`.
`GH_TOKEN` was used as the environment variable passed from `${{ github.token }}`.
The wiki commit author was configured as `github-actions[bot]` with the standard noreply bot email.
The generated wiki commit message format is `Update Translation Units list for ${{ github.sha }}`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level or function-matching tactics were changed in this PR.
The decomp-relevant tactic was process automation: keep the Translation Units wiki page synchronized with the build's `GALE01_report` instead of relying on manual regeneration.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The dependency install step should run `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt`; optionally dependencies could be separated out to reduce CI dependency scope.
review_feedback.outcome: Final diff uses sparse checkout for both `tools` and `reqs` and installs with `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt`.

review_feedback.author: reviewer/commenter
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the workflow had been tested on a fork and discussed uncertainty around non-user tokens for org automation.
review_feedback.outcome: Author confirmed no extra token was needed, validated the workflow with `${{ github.token }}` and `contents: write`, and linked a fork wiki/check run.

follow_up_queries: Find future PRs that modify `tools/wiki_tu.py` and verify they remain compatible with the CI `update-wiki` job.
Check whether `reqs/misc.txt` grows large enough to justify the reviewer's suggested split for wiki-generator dependencies.
Audit whether other generated wiki or documentation pages still require manual regeneration and could reuse this artifact-driven CI pattern.
Verify that wiki branch naming remains `master`; if GitHub wiki behavior changes, update the push target accordingly.
Confirm that `GALE01_report` artifact naming and path `report/report.json` remain stable across build workflow changes.
